steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank completes the scholars' argument. Their reasoning is: the Harlem Renaissance “continues to shape culture in the United States today,” therefore it “never really ended.” The completion must capture that logical link — ongoing influence means the movement itself continues.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — if a cultural movement has both literary and nonliterary innovations, then it is likely to be influential.: Describes what makes movements influential, not what makes them ongoing. The scholars' argument is about continuation, not influence type. ✗
  • Choice B — if a cultural movement is very innovative, then it is unlikely to be able to sustain itself for long.: Directly contradicts the scholars, who argue the Harlem Renaissance did sustain itself. ✗
  • Choice C — if literary works of a cultural movement are influential, then it doesn't matter whether the nonliterary works are.: Introduces an irrelevant literary vs. nonliterary distinction the scholars never make. ✗
  • Choice D — if the influence of a cultural movement continues, then in some sense the movement itself continues.: Exactly matches the scholars' logic: continuing influence = continuing movement. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
